skills.name: Figma Mastery
skills.description: Expert-level proficiency in Figma including components, variants, auto-layout, design tokens, prototyping, branching, and collaborative workflows. The industry-standard design tool for product and brand design.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can create basic frames, shapes, and text. Understands layers and simple prototyping. Can navigate and comment on shared files.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds reusable components with variants and auto-layout. Creates interactive prototypes with transitions. Uses styles and shared libraries effectively. Comfortable with developer handoff workflows.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects complex component systems with design tokens. Manages branching workflows and multi-file libraries. Builds advanced prototypes with variables and conditional logic. Optimizes file performance for large-scale projects.

skills.name: UI & Visual Design
skills.description: Craft-level proficiency in visual design fundamentals — typography, color theory, layout, spacing, hierarchy, composition, and pixel-perfect execution. The ability to create polished, aesthetically refined interfaces that are both beautiful and functional.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic typography, color, and layout principles. Can create clean, functional UI following established guidelines.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Strong command of visual hierarchy, spacing systems, and typographic scale. Creates polished, consistent interfaces with attention to detail. Develops cohesive visual languages for products.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Sets visual direction for products and brands. Defines typographic systems, color palettes, and spacing frameworks. Creates pixel-perfect designs that balance aesthetics with usability at scale.

skills.name: UX Design Process
skills.description: End-to-end user experience design methodology — from discovery and research through ideation, wireframing, prototyping, testing, and iteration. The structured approach to solving user problems and designing intuitive experiences.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands the design thinking framework. Can create basic wireframes and user flows. Participates in usability testing sessions.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads end-to-end design processes independently. Conducts user research, creates journey maps, builds prototypes, and iterates based on feedback. Balances user needs with business requirements.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines and evolves design processes for teams. Integrates research, data, and business strategy into design decisions. Mentors others on UX methodology. Drives organizational adoption of user-centered design.

skills.name: Interaction Design
skills.description: Designing how users interact with digital products — micro-interactions, transitions, animations, gestures, state changes, and feedback patterns. Creating responsive, intuitive behaviors that make interfaces feel natural and delightful.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic interaction patterns (hover, click, scroll). Can specify simple transitions and state changes in designs.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs thoughtful micro-interactions and transition systems. Creates interactive prototypes that communicate complex behaviors. Understands platform-specific interaction conventions (web vs. mobile).
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines interaction design systems and motion principles for products. Creates novel interaction patterns for complex workflows. Balances delight with performance and accessibility.

skills.name: Information Architecture
skills.description: Structuring and organizing content, features, and navigation in digital products so users can find what they need and complete tasks efficiently. Includes site mapping, content hierarchy, taxonomy design, and navigation patterns.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can create basic sitemaps and navigation structures. Understands content grouping and labeling principles.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs scalable information architectures for complex products. Conducts card sorting and tree testing. Creates effective navigation systems and content hierarchies.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects information systems for multi-product suites and platforms. Defines taxonomies and metadata structures. Optimizes IA for findability, scalability, and cross-product consistency.

skills.name: User Research
skills.description: Planning, conducting, and synthesizing user research to understand behaviors, needs, and motivations. Includes qualitative methods (interviews, usability testing, contextual inquiry) and quantitative methods (surveys, analytics, A/B testing).

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can conduct basic usability tests and user interviews with a guide. Understands the difference between qualitative and quantitative methods.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Plans and executes research studies independently. Synthesizes findings into actionable insights, personas, and journey maps. Selects appropriate methods for different research questions.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds research programs and repositories. Designs complex mixed-methods studies. Influences product strategy through research insights. Mentors teams on research methodology and integration.

skills.name: Prototyping
skills.description: Creating interactive representations of designs at varying fidelity levels — from paper sketches and clickable wireframes to high-fidelity interactive prototypes. Used to test concepts, communicate design intent, and validate ideas before development.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Creates basic clickable prototypes with simple navigation and page transitions in Figma or similar tools.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds complex interactive prototypes with conditional logic, variables, and realistic data. Uses prototypes to test hypotheses and communicate nuanced interactions to developers.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Creates production-quality prototypes that closely simulate the final experience. Uses code-based prototyping when needed. Leverages AI tools to accelerate prototype creation.

skills.name: Design System Management
skills.description: Building, governing, and evolving design systems — the shared libraries of components, patterns, tokens, and guidelines that ensure consistency and efficiency across products. Includes component architecture, token management, documentation, and adoption strategies.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands what design systems are and can use existing component libraries effectively. Follows established patterns and guidelines.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Contributes to design system development. Creates and maintains components with proper variants and documentation. Ensures designs adhere to the system.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects and governs design systems at scale. Defines token structures, component APIs, and contribution workflows. Drives adoption across teams. Collaborates with engineering on component implementation and design-to-code fidelity.

skills.name: Data Visualization Design
skills.description: Designing clear, effective visual representations of data — dashboards, charts, graphs, tables, and data-heavy interfaces. Translating complex datasets into understandable, actionable visual information.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can design basic charts, tables, and simple data displays. Understands fundamental data visualization principles.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs comprehensive dashboards and data-intensive interfaces. Selects appropriate chart types for different data stories. Balances information density with readability.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Creates innovative data visualization systems for complex, multi-dimensional datasets. Defines data display patterns and frameworks for enterprise products. Designs for real-time data and large-scale analytics platforms.

skills.name: Mobile Design
skills.description: Designing user experiences specifically for mobile platforms (iOS and Android). Includes understanding platform-specific conventions, touch interaction patterns, responsive design, and the unique constraints and opportunities of mobile interfaces.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic mobile UI patterns and platform guidelines (iOS HIG, Material Design). Can adapt web designs for mobile screens.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs native mobile experiences with platform-appropriate patterns. Understands touch targets, gestures, and mobile-specific workflows. Creates responsive designs that work across device sizes.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines mobile design strategies and platform-specific design systems. Designs for complex mobile workflows including offline states, notifications, and device capabilities. Expert in both iOS and Android design conventions.

skills.name: Accessibility & Inclusive Design
skills.description: Designing products that are usable by people of all abilities. Includes understanding WCAG guidelines, color contrast requirements, screen reader compatibility, keyboard navigation, and inclusive design principles.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic accessibility principles — color contrast, text size, alt text. Aware of WCAG guidelines.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs with accessibility as a core consideration. Ensures proper contrast ratios, keyboard navigation, focus states, and screen reader compatibility. Tests designs against WCAG AA standards.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Champions accessibility across the organization. Defines accessibility standards and testing processes. Designs for diverse abilities including cognitive, motor, and sensory disabilities. Ensures WCAG AAA compliance where appropriate.

skills.name: Wireframing & Sketching
skills.description: Creating low-fidelity representations of interfaces to explore layout, content hierarchy, and user flows before investing in visual design. Includes hand sketching, digital wireframing, and rapid ideation techniques.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can create basic wireframes showing layout and content placement. Uses wireframes to communicate simple page structures.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Rapidly produces wireframes to explore multiple solutions. Uses wireframes effectively in stakeholder discussions. Creates annotated wireframes that communicate interaction behavior.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Uses wireframing as a strategic tool for rapid exploration and stakeholder alignment. Facilitates collaborative sketching sessions. Knows when to wireframe vs. jump to higher fidelity.

skills.name: Usability Testing
skills.description: Planning, conducting, and analyzing usability tests to evaluate how well users can complete tasks with a product. Includes moderated and unmoderated testing, task design, participant recruitment, and insight synthesis.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can facilitate basic usability test sessions using a script. Records observations and identifies obvious usability issues.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Plans and conducts usability studies independently. Designs effective test tasks and scenarios. Synthesizes findings into prioritized recommendations. Uses remote and in-person testing tools.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs rigorous usability testing programs. Combines qualitative observations with quantitative metrics (task success rate, time on task, SUS scores). Integrates usability testing into continuous product development cycles.

skills.name: Design Leadership & Team Management
skills.description: Leading, mentoring, and managing design teams. Includes setting design vision, building team culture, conducting design critiques, managing career development, recruiting design talent, and elevating design as a strategic function within the organization.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Provides constructive feedback to peers. Participates actively in design critiques. Mentors junior designers informally.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages a small design team. Conducts regular critiques and one-on-ones. Hires and onboards designers. Establishes team processes and quality standards.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads a design organization with multiple leads and teams. Shapes design vision and strategy at the executive level. Builds design culture and talent pipelines. Elevates design as a strategic business function.

skills.name: Design Critique & Feedback
skills.description: Giving and receiving constructive design feedback effectively. Includes facilitating structured critique sessions, articulating design rationale, evaluating designs against user needs and business goals, and fostering a culture of continuous improvement.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can articulate basic design opinions with reasoning. Receives feedback openly and iterates accordingly.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Gives clear, actionable feedback grounded in design principles and user needs. Facilitates productive critique sessions. Separates personal preference from objective evaluation.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads organizational critique culture. Calibrates quality standards across teams. Provides feedback that elevates both the work and the designer. Balances craft, user needs, and business constraints in evaluations.

skills.name: Brand Identity & Visual Language
skills.description: Creating and maintaining cohesive brand identities — logos, color palettes, typography systems, imagery styles, and visual guidelines that define how a company presents itself. Ensuring brand consistency across all touchpoints.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can apply existing brand guidelines consistently. Understands basic brand elements (logo usage, color palette, typography).
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Develops brand visual systems including style guides, asset libraries, and usage documentation. Evolves brand identity while maintaining consistency.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Creates brand identities from scratch. Defines comprehensive visual languages that scale across products, marketing, and communications. Leads brand evolution and repositioning efforts.

skills.name: Marketing Campaign Design
skills.description: Designing visual assets for marketing campaigns across digital and physical channels — social media, email, paid ads, landing pages, events, and print. Balancing brand consistency with channel-specific optimization and conversion goals.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Creates basic marketing assets following brand guidelines. Understands common formats for social media, email, and web.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs comprehensive campaign assets across multiple channels. Adapts creative for different platforms and formats. Understands conversion principles and A/B testing for creative optimization.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads creative direction for large-scale marketing campaigns. Develops campaign visual systems that scale. Integrates performance data into creative decisions. Manages creative output across internal and external teams.

skills.name: Presentation & Deck Design
skills.description: Creating compelling, visually polished presentations for sales, executive communication, investor relations, and conferences. Includes slide design, data visualization for presentations, visual storytelling, and template system creation.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Creates clean, readable slides following a template. Understands basic slide layout and content hierarchy.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs compelling presentation decks with strong visual storytelling. Creates reusable templates and slide libraries. Visualizes data effectively for presentation contexts.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs high-stakes executive and investor presentations. Creates scalable presentation systems for sales enablement. Translates complex narratives into clear, persuasive visual stories.

skills.name: Web Design & Landing Pages
skills.description: Designing websites and landing pages that balance brand expression, user experience, and conversion optimization. Includes responsive design, web-specific interaction patterns, SEO-friendly layouts, and understanding of web development constraints.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can design basic web pages with proper layout, typography, and responsive considerations. Understands fundamental web conventions.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs conversion-optimized landing pages and multi-page websites. Understands responsive breakpoints, web performance considerations, and SEO-friendly design practices. Works with web builders (Webflow, Elementor).
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads web design strategy and information architecture for complex websites. Optimizes for conversion through data-driven design decisions. Defines web design systems that scale across multiple properties.

skills.name: Motion Design & Animation
skills.description: Creating animations, motion graphics, and video content that bring brands and interfaces to life. Includes UI animation principles, marketing video production, storyboarding, and motion-based storytelling.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic animation principles (timing, easing). Can create simple UI animations and transitions. Familiar with After Effects or similar tools.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Creates polished motion graphics for marketing and product contexts. Develops storyboards and animated sequences. Produces social media video content and product demos.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines motion design language and animation systems for brands. Directs complex animation projects. Creates high-quality motion content across marketing, product, and brand channels.

skills.name: AI-Assisted Design Workflows
skills.description: Leveraging AI tools to accelerate and enhance the design process — including generative AI for image creation, AI-assisted prototyping, design-to-code tools, research synthesis, and workflow automation. Understanding how to integrate AI into design practice effectively.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Aware of AI design tools and their capabilities. Has experimented with generative image tools (Midjourney, DALL·E) or AI design assistants.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Actively uses AI tools in daily workflow — generative AI for ideation, AI-assisted design-to-code (Figma Make, Cursor), LLMs for research synthesis and content generation. Understands strengths and limitations of AI tools.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Establishes AI-forward design workflows for teams. Builds AI-augmented design pipelines that significantly accelerate output. Evaluates and adopts new AI tools strategically. Helps define how AI changes the design process organizationally.

skills.name: Generative AI for Creative Production
skills.description: Using generative AI tools (Midjourney, DALL·E, Adobe Firefly, Stable Diffusion) to create, enhance, and iterate on visual content for brand, marketing, and product design. Includes prompt engineering for visual output, style control, and integrating AI-generated assets into professional workflows.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can generate basic images using text prompts. Understands the capabilities and limitations of major generative AI platforms.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Uses generative AI tools effectively for ideation, asset creation, and visual exploration. Controls style, composition, and quality through advanced prompting. Integrates AI-generated assets into professional design workflows.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds creative production pipelines using generative AI. Combines multiple AI tools for complex visual outputs. Establishes best practices for AI-assisted creative production at team scale. Understands ethical and legal considerations of AI-generated content.

skills.name: Adobe Creative Suite
skills.description: Proficiency in Adobe's design applications — Photoshop for image editing, Illustrator for vector graphics, InDesign for print layouts, After Effects for animation, and Premiere Pro for video editing.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can perform basic tasks in Photoshop and Illustrator — image editing, simple vector creation, and basic layouts.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Proficient across multiple Adobe applications. Creates professional-quality graphics, illustrations, photo compositions, and print layouts. Comfortable with After Effects for basic motion design.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Expert-level proficiency across the full Adobe suite. Creates complex compositions, illustrations, and motion graphics. Develops efficient production workflows using Adobe tools. Mentors others on advanced techniques.

skills.name: Design Communication & Stakeholder Management
skills.description: Articulating design rationale, presenting design work to stakeholders, securing alignment on design direction, and managing expectations across product, engineering, and business teams. Translating design decisions into business language.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can explain basic design choices and present work to immediate team. Responds to feedback constructively.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Presents design work confidently to cross-functional stakeholders. Articulates the 'why' behind design decisions in terms of user needs and business impact. Navigates conflicting stakeholder feedback effectively.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Presents design strategy to executive leadership. Drives alignment across multiple teams and senior stakeholders. Translates design outcomes into business metrics and ROI language. Influences product strategy through design storytelling.

skills.name: Designing for Complex Systems
skills.description: Creating intuitive user experiences for inherently complex products — enterprise SaaS, data-heavy platforms, cybersecurity tools, financial systems, and multi-layered workflows. The ability to simplify complexity without losing necessary depth.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can follow established patterns for complex interfaces. Understands the challenge of balancing simplicity with feature depth.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs clear, usable interfaces for complex workflows and data-intensive products. Effectively uses progressive disclosure, contextual information, and smart defaults to manage complexity.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Expert at taming complexity — transforms sophisticated enterprise workflows and technical systems into elegant, intuitive experiences. Defines design frameworks for complex product domains. Balances power-user depth with approachability.

skills.name: Conversion & Growth Design
skills.description: Designing user experiences optimized for conversion, retention, and growth metrics. Includes CRO (conversion rate optimization), A/B test design, funnel optimization, pricing page design, onboarding flows, and data-driven design iteration.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic conversion principles — clear CTAs, reduced friction, trust signals. Aware of A/B testing concepts.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs conversion-optimized experiences based on data and user psychology. Creates effective A/B test variants. Optimizes funnels, onboarding flows, and monetization surfaces. Makes data-informed design decisions.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads growth-focused design strategy. Builds experimentation frameworks for rapid, consistent testing. Deeply understands user psychology and behavioral economics as applied to conversion. Drives measurable business impact through design optimization.

skills.name: Design-to-Development Handoff
skills.description: Preparing and delivering design specifications to engineering teams for implementation. Includes creating detailed specs, annotating edge cases, defining responsive behavior, QA-ing implementation against designs, and collaborating with developers throughout the build process.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can prepare basic design specs with measurements and assets. Understands the concept of developer handoff.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Creates comprehensive design specifications including responsive behavior, edge cases, error states, and interaction details. Reviews implementation and provides clear feedback. Understands basic front-end constraints.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds efficient handoff workflows and systems. Understands front-end architecture well enough to design for implementation efficiency. Uses design-to-code tools to accelerate handoff. Ensures design-to-code fidelity at scale.

skills.name: Cross-Functional Design Collaboration
skills.description: Working effectively with product managers, engineers, data analysts, researchers, marketers, and other stakeholders throughout the design process. Includes facilitating workshops, participating in agile ceremonies, and integrating design into cross-functional workflows.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Participates in cross-functional meetings and incorporates feedback from other disciplines. Communicates design progress clearly.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Drives productive collaboration with PM, engineering, and data teams. Facilitates design workshops and co-creation sessions. Navigates competing priorities across functions.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Shapes cross-functional processes and team dynamics. Partners with leadership across functions to drive product strategy. Builds bridges between design, engineering, and business organizations.

skills.name: Design Portfolio & Case Studies
skills.description: Creating and maintaining a professional design portfolio that effectively communicates design process, thinking, and outcomes. Includes writing case studies, documenting design decisions, and presenting work in a compelling narrative format.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Has a basic portfolio showing completed design projects with descriptions of the work.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Maintains a polished portfolio with detailed case studies showing process, reasoning, and measurable outcomes. Tailors portfolio presentation for different audiences.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Portfolio demonstrates strategic impact and leadership. Case studies show how design drove business outcomes. Uses portfolio as a tool for thought leadership and team recruiting.
